commits.message: chore(CL-ugwe.7): add mcp migration debt audit scripts
chore(CL-ugwe.7): record mcp migration debt evidence
chore(CL-ugwe.7): refresh mcp migration debt evidence
fix(CL-ugwe.7): address codex adversarial findings in audit-mcp-migration-debt.py

tests.command: UV_CACHE_DIR=/private/tmp/uv-cache uv run --no-project python /Users/malte/.agents/skills/beads/scripts/implementation_artifacts.py validate --manifest .beads/runs/a65733fb-1895-447c-8469-fe3240ee168a/implementation_manifest.json --evidence .beads/runs/a65733fb-1895-447c-8469-fe3240ee168a/evidence_ledger.json --bead-id CL-ugwe.7

scope_changes: Criterion 2 could be audited and ranked, but the top five installed files could not be patched in this meta worktree because their owning cognovis-core source files are outside the writable roots.
Filed follow-up bead CL-4sdb to track the blocked source-repo remediation.
downstream_impacts: The repo now contains a reproducible audit script for MCP migration debt on installed skill and agent surfaces.
The repo now contains a doctor-style wrapper that exits non-zero when A-class findings remain.
The committed audit report ranks the top five remediation targets for the owning cognovis-core source repo.
Follow-up bead CL-4sdb tracks the blocked source-repo patch set.

Per Codex adversarial review fix: the classifier now (a) only downgrades to D when the negation marker is the primary intent of the line — lines containing both a negation marker and an explicit invocation recipe retain their A/B classification — and (b) no longer treats Markdown blockquote markers (>) or Markdown table pipes (|) as shell recipe indicators. The committed audit report at docs/audit/mcp-migration-debt-2026-05-29.md is therefore slightly stale (A+B counts shift by a few entries per file) and will be refreshed by the planned 'refresh mcp migration debt evidence' commit.
